Architectural change vs parent: the parent walked the array in single BLOCK
tiles per grid-stride iteration. That leaves only one outstanding vector-load

memory-bound op. Here each program processes UNROLL independent contiguous

groups (wide 128-bit global_load_dwordx4 bursts) back-to-back before any
sigmoid math, keeping a much deeper memory queue in flight per wavefront and
saturating HBM bandwidth. fp32 math, fp16 store.

from __future__ import annotations

import torch
import triton
import triton.language as tl

                    SUB: tl.constexpr, UNROLL: tl.constexpr, GRID: tl.constexpr):

    TILE = SUB * UNROLL
    stride = GRID * TILE
    base = pid * TILE
